primary.justification: The chapter's entire structure is built around explaining climate as a key natural phenomenon — its types across India, its determinants (latitude, altitude, proximity to sea, winds, topography), the monsoon mechanism, climate-linked disasters, and climate change. Every named section develops some aspect of explaining climate as a natural phenomenon. Remove this competency and the chapter has no organising purpose.

The chapter contains a dedicated, architecturally distinct section 'Climate Change' that analyses human-caused global warming, India's rising temperatures, and evaluates specific mitigation measures (renewable energy, tree planting, energy efficiency, sustainable lifestyles) including their tension with economic growth goals — directly meeting C-6.3's demand to analyse Indian perspectives and efforts towards conservation in the context of global climate change. This section could stand alone as a learning unit.

The chapter contains a dedicated section 'Climate and our Lives' that directly correlates climate with patterns of livelihoods — wheat cultivation in northern plains, rice and spices on the western coast, and the socioeconomic consequences of monsoon failure (agricultural disruption, food price rises, migration of agricultural labourers, industrial dependence on water). This section is architecturally distinct and devoted to the livelihood–climate correlation that C-6.4 demands.
